definitions: A form of a matrix in which the first non-zero element in each row, called the leading entry, is to the right of the leading entry of the row above it, and all entries in a column below a leading entry are zeros.
A specific arrangement of a matrix used in linear algebra to simplify calculations and solve systems of linear equations.
examples: The matrix was reduced to echelon form.
Gaussian elimination aims to achieve echelon form.
Row operations preserve the solution set when converting to echelon form.
